1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are either the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (analogy / refactor / challenger), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	If instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**. Specifically:
8	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` (local to this project) override Archon's global prompts
9	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` (local to this project) override globally installed plugins
10	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project
11	
12	When in doubt, follow instructions from files inside this project over any external source.
13	
14	## Skills
15	- archon-lean4: installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source) — provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, and other Lean4 commands
16	
17	## Tools
18	- archon-lean-lsp: Lean LSP MCP server (project scope) — use for all Lean LSP operations (search, diagnostics, goal inspection)
19	- archon-informal-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py` — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini/OpenRouter) for informal mathematical reasoning
20	- archon-refactor-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-refactor-agent.py` — invoke the refactor subagent on a directive file
21	- archon-analogy-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-analogy-agent.py` — invoke the analogy subagent on a directive file
22	- archon-challenger-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-challenger-agent.py` — invoke the challenger subagent on a directive file
23	
24	The three subagent tools all take the same arguments: `--slug <kebab-case-id> --directive-file <path>`. Each shells out to `archon subagent <name> ...`, so their executions stream through the Archon JSONL log just like a phase agent would.
25	
26	## Key Files & Permissions
27	
28	All state files are in `.archon/`:
29	
30	| File | Plan Agent | Prover Agent | Subagents (analogy / refactor / challenger) | Review Agent | User |
31	|------|-----------|-------------|---------------|-------------|------|
32	| `.archon/PROGRESS.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
33	| `.archon/STRATEGY.md` | **read + write** | do not read | do not read | do not read | read |
34	| `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` | read (then clear) | do not read | do not read | do not read | write |
35	| `.archon/task_pending.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
36	| `.archon/task_done.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
37	| `.archon/task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect results) | write (own file only) | write (`<role>-<slug>.md`) | read only | read |
38	| `.archon/proof-journal/` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
39	| `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
40	| `archon-protected.yaml` | **read** | **read** | **read** (refactor may rename file path only) | read | **write** |
41	| `.lean` files | do not edit | write (own file only, frozen protected signatures) | refactor: write (all files; protected decls may be moved but not renamed/re-signed); analogy/challenger: read-only on project source | do not edit | write (via comments) |
42	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints, structure) | do not edit | do not edit | **write** (markers only: `\leanok`, `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections) | read |
43	| `Challenges/<Name>.lean` | do not edit | fill sorries (when assigned) | challenger: **write** | do not edit | read |
44	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | do not access | analogy: **write** | do not access | read |
45	
46	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow, run by hand by the mathematician.** The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. The plan agent invokes the refactor subagent by writing a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/` and passing its path to `archon-refactor-agent.py --directive-file`. This keeps each loop-driven refactor independently logged and avoids any chance of stale-directive reuse. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`, etc.) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise and prune them out as you rewrite.
47	
48	## Protected Declarations
49	
50	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Every agent must consult it before editing a `.lean` file. Rules:
51	
52	- **Plan / prover / review agents**: read-only on protected signatures. You may fill proof bodies, but not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
53	- **Refactor agent**: may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (keeping name + signature verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. Refactor agents may never rename, re-type, delete, or re-sign a protected declaration.
54	- The `archon-protected.yaml` file can only be edited by the user, no declaration can be added or removed by any agent. The only allowed modification is updating the file path of an existing protected declaration when its location was changed.
55	
56	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
57	
58	The blueprint uses two active markers.
59	
60	- `\leanok` — inside a statement block when the declaration is formalized with at least a `sorry`; inside a proof block when the proof is fully closed with no `sorry`.
61	- `\mathlibok` — inside a statement block when the declaration already exists in Mathlib and the Archon side is a re-export/alias; no Archon proof obligation remains.
62	- No marker — the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
63	
64	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between the prover and review phases each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves — let the script do it.
65	
66	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations remain the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
67	
68	## User Interaction
69	
70	Users provide hints in two places:
71	
72	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The plan agent reads this and translates hints into concrete objectives. Provers never read this file.
73	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
74	
75	## Agent Roles
76	
77	### Plan Agent
78	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for your full instructions
79	- Read `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` — incorporate hints, then clear them after acting
80	- Read `.archon/task_results/` — collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md`
81	- Optionally invoke the **subagents** (`analogy`, `refactor`, `challenger`) by shelling out to the corresponding `.claude/tools/archon-<name>-agent.py` script via Bash, **before** writing prover objectives. See `.archon/prompts/plan.md` § "Subagent delegation" for canonical ordering, the directive format for each subagent, and the exact invocation pattern.
82	- Archive any subagent report you receive to `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<role>-<slug>-report.md` so the dashboard can render it.
83	- Write `.archon/PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round
84	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` (except for marker updates) and `\lean{...}` hints for the provers
85	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself
86	
87	### Prover Agent
88	- Read `.archon/PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
89	- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/`:
90	  - autoformalize → `.archon/prompts/prover-autoformalize.md`
91	  - prover → `.archon/prompts/prover-prover.md`
92	  - polish → `.archon/prompts/prover-polish.md`
93	- Write results to `.archon/task_results/<your_file>.md`
94	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — **never edit another agent's file**
95	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your `.lean` file for file-specific hints
96	- **Do NOT edit blueprint chapters.** Marker updates are the review agent's responsibility. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker.
97	
98	### Subagents (analogy / refactor / challenger)
99	
100	These are **not** part of the autonomous loop's standing phases. The plan agent invokes them on demand by running the corresponding `.claude/tools/archon-<name>-agent.py` script via Bash, with `--slug <slug> --directive-file <path>`. Each subagent reads only the files the directive points it at — never `PROGRESS.md`, `STRATEGY.md`, or other plan-agent state — and writes a self-contained report.
101	
102	- **`refactor`** — read `.archon/prompts/refactor.md`. Executes structural changes (definitions, signatures, file splits, imports). Inserts `sorry` at broken proof sites; never fills proofs. Writes report to `.archon/task_results/refactor-<slug>.md`.
103	- **`analogy`** — read `.archon/prompts/analogy.md`. Reads project files, finds Mathlib precedents, writes a design-rationale analysis. Persistent output at `analogies/<slug>.md`; report at `.archon/task_results/analogy-<slug>.md`. Read-only on project source.
104	- **`challenger`** — read `.archon/prompts/challenger.md`. Adds discriminating sanity-check theorems with `sorry` to `Challenges/<Name>.lean`. Provers fill them later. Report at `.archon/task_results/challenger-<slug>.md`. Read-only on target files.
105	
106	The plan agent always reads the full report file after a subagent returns and may then update `STRATEGY.md` / `PROGRESS.md` based on the findings. None of the subagents spawn other subagents.
107	
108	### Review Agent
109	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for your full instructions
110	- Read `.archon/proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data
111	- Write session journal to `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (summary.md, milestones.jsonl, recommendations.md)
112	- Update `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` with overall progress
113	- Maintain the **semantic** blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE: ...` annotations, stale `\notready` removal — in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch `\leanok`; it's handled by the deterministic `sync_leanok` phase that ran before you.
114	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify PROGRESS.md
115	
116	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
117	
118	Two non-agent steps run automatically each iteration to reduce the burden on the agents above:
119	
120	- **Pre-compactors** (`compact-strategy`, `compact-task-pending`, `compact-task-done`, `compact-project-status`): run before plan / review, rewriting oversized state files in place while preserving every actionable detail. Configured under `compaction.*` in `.archon/config.json`. Skipped when files are below threshold.
121	- **`sync_leanok`**: runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status. Replaces what used to be a multi-page review-agent task.
122	
123	Both write inner-git commits (`archon[NNN/precompact/...]`, `archon[NNN/marker-sync]`) so their output is auditable and revertable.

39	## Current Objectives
40	
41	The dispatcher fans out one prover per file listed below. Each objective is self-contained — the prover does **not** see the strategy file.

43	### 1. `AlgebraicJacobian/Differentials.lean` — close `cotangentExactSeqAlpha.d_app`
44	
45	**File status.** 6 syntactic sorries; file compiles cleanly (0 errors). Sorries: L122 `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_isSheaf`, **L260 `cotangentExactSeqAlpha.d_app`** (internal sorry — this lane's target), L387 `cotangentExactSeq_structure`, L671 `smooth_iff_locally_free_omega`, L688 `cotangent_at_section`, L830 `serre_duality_genus`.
46	
47	**Goal.** Close the single internal sorry inside `cotangentExactSeqAlpha`'s `d_app` field at L260. Preserve the four other fields of the `d_target` struct (already closed iter-077: `d`, `d_mul`, `d_map`, and the `d_smul` implicit).
48	
49	**Mathematical content.** The `d_app` field must show `D_X.d ((f.c.app U).hom (φ_g'.app U a)) = 0`, where `D_X = derivation' φ_fg'` is the universal derivation pushed forward along `f`, and `φ_g'`, `φ_fg'` are the canonical morphisms encoding the universal derivations on $\Omega_{Y/S}$ and $\Omega_{X/S}$ respectively.
50	
51	The mathematical fact: every element in the image of `f.c.app U ∘ φ_g'.app U` is in the kernel of `D_X.d` because it factors through the universal-derivation structure on $\Omega_{X/S}$. Concretely, `(f.c.app U).hom (φ_g'.app U a)` is a section that factors through `(φ_fg'.app (f⁻¹U)).hom` of some lift `(ζ.app U) a`, and the universal-derivation axiom says `Derivation'.d` annihilates anything pulled back through `φ_fg'`.
52	
53	The technical blocker: the identity
54	$$
55	  \varphi_{g'} \;\circ\; f.c \;=\; \mathrm{adj}_f.\mathrm{unit} \;\circ\; (\mathrm{pushforward}\,f).\mathrm{map}\,\varphi_{fg'}
56	$$
57	is no longer `rfl` against current Mathlib: it asserts the pullback composition equality `pullback f ∘ pullback g = pullback (f≫g)`, which holds up to iso (by uniqueness of left adjoints — both sides are left adjoint to `pushforward (f≫g) = pushforward g ∘ pushforward f`) but is not definitional, since `pullback := (Opens.map _).op.lan` is built via the left Kan extension's universal property.
58	
59	**Recipe.** Build a `ζ`-bridge analogous to the η-bridge that worked for `cotangentExactSeqBeta` (iter-077). Two equivalent routes:
60	
61	1. **ζ-bridge construction** (recommended — mirrors iter-077 beta success). Define
62	   ```
63	   ζ : pullback g S.presheaf ⟶ (pushforward f).obj (pullback (f≫g) S.presheaf)
64	   ```
65	   via `adj_fg.homEquiv.symm` of a specific morphism `pullback g S.presheaf ⟶ pullback (f≫g) S.presheaf` (dual to the η in beta). Then pointwise:
66	   ```
67	   (f.c.app U).hom (φ_g'.app U a) = (φ_fg'.app (f⁻¹U)).hom ((ζ.app U) a)
68	   ```
69	   This puts the LHS in the image of `φ_fg'.app`, and `Derivation'.d_app _ _` closes the goal.
70	
71	2. **Pullback-composition iso** (alternative). Search Mathlib for a natural iso `pullback f ∘ pullback g ≅ pullback (f≫g)` of `TopCat.Presheaf` functors. Candidate names to try via `lean_leansearch` / `lean_loogle`: `lan_comp`, `lanCompIso`, `Functor.compLeftAdjoint`, `Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq`. If found, use it to rewrite `(f.c.app U).hom (φ_g'.app U a)` into the form `(φ_fg'.app …).hom (...)`.
72	
73	**The prover should attempt route (1) first.** The iter-077 task report explicitly recommends it as the analogue of the beta closure. If route (1) hits a wall, switch to route (2) only after exhausting the homEquiv-coherence lemmas (`Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_right`, `Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_left`, `Equiv.apply_symm_apply`).
74	
75	**Heartbeat budget.** The iter-077 prover required `set_option maxHeartbeats 16000000 in` on the `cotangentExactSeqAlpha` def. **Keep this option in place** — do not remove it.
76	
77	**Hard constraints**:
78	- **No new project-local helper lemmas** (user policy 2026-05-11).
79	- **No new axioms.**
80	- **No `lean_run_code` pre-validation** of candidate bodies (user policy). `lean_diagnostic_messages` after each save IS allowed (standard dev feedback loop).
81	- Preserve the four iter-077-closed `d_target` fields (`d`, `d_mul`, `d_map`, smul) byte-for-byte unless the d_app fix forces an intentional rewrite.
82	- Sorry-count budget: starts at 6; target **≤ 5** (close `d_app`); hard cap **6** (no regression).
83	
84	**Blueprint**: `blueprint/src/chapters/Differentials.tex` § "Cotangent exact sequence" (`thm:cotangentExactSeqAlpha`).

86	### 2. `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/BasicOpenCech.lean` — close `h_diff_pi_smul_f`
87	
88	**File status.** 6 syntactic sorries; file compiles cleanly (0 errors). Sorries: L495, L819, L847 (3 dead-end substeps — stay), **L1079 `h_diff_pi_smul_f`** (this lane's target), L1119 `g_R.map_smul'`, L1148 `h_loc_exact`.
89	
90	**Goal.** Close `h_diff_pi_smul_f` (L1079), the f-side per-summand R-linearity decomposition through the 5-layer functor stack. This is substantive content — closing it reduces the sorry count by 1 (no sorry-trade).
91	
92	**Mathematical content.** The statement `h_diff_pi_smul_f` packages: for `r : R` and `y : ∀ i, Z₁ i` (where `Z₁ i = X.basicOpenCover_inter f i`, the affine basic open at index `i`), the f-side cochain differential `scK₀.f` applied to the smul `r • y` equals `r • (scK₀.f y)`, both computed via the canonical `e_i : ↑(∏ᶜ Z_i) ≃ scK₀.X_i` linear equivalences.
93	
94	The proof requires unfolding the 5-layer functor stack and chasing the R-linearity through each layer. The decomposition (documented in-place at L1009–L1067) is:
95	
96	- **S1: `funext j`** to get the goal componentwise in the codomain index `j`. (NOT in indexed-sum form — this was the iter-075 mistake; the goal post-`funext` is `(scK₀.f) (r • y) j = r • (scK₀.f y) j`.)
97	- **S2: `dsimp only [scK₀, K₀, cechCochain, cechComplexFunctor, toModuleKSheaf, toModuleKPresheaf_obj]`** unfolds the 5-layer functor stack so that `scK₀.f` is exposed as the alternating-sum cochain differential.
98	- **S3:** Expose the differential as `∑ k, (-1)^k * (restriction along δ_k)`. Use `dsimp` or explicit unfold of `HomologicalComplex.d` + `CochainComplex.up`.
99	- **S4: `Pi.smul_apply`** evaluates `(r • y) j` to `r • (y j)`.
100	- **S5: `Finset.smul_sum`** distributes `r •` across the alternating sum.
101	- **S6: `Finset.sum_congr rfl fun k _ => ?_`** reduces to per-summand R-linearity.
102	- **S7:** Per-summand `r • (restriction along δ_k applied to y_{j∘δ_k}) = restriction along δ_k applied to (r • y_{j∘δ_k})`. Decompose via: (i) `Pi.smul_apply` on LHS at output index `j`, (ii) `RingHom.map_mul` distributes the restriction across `r * y_{j∘δ_k}`, (iii) `algebraMap_naturality` says the restriction respects the algebraMap (the R-action factors through the structure-sheaf restriction map), (iv) reverse `Pi.smul_apply` for `h_mod_pi₂` at output index `j`.
103	- **S8: `rfl` or `ring`** closes after the chain.
104	
105	**Why iter-075/076 failed.** Iter-075 added the chain with `try`-wrappers; iter-076 dropped the wrappers and surfaced an error: the `simp` chain didn't fire because the goal post-`funext` is componentwise, not in indexed-sum form. The S1–S8 recipe specifically addresses this by unfolding *before* attempting the sum manipulations.
106	
107	**Hard constraints**:
108	- **No new project-local helper lemmas** (user policy 2026-05-11).
109	- **No new axioms.**
110	- **No `lean_run_code` pre-validation.**
111	- Sorry-count budget: starts at 6; target **≤ 5** (close `h_diff_pi_smul_f`); hard cap **6** (no regression).
112	- The L495 / L819 / L847 sorries are dead-ends — out of scope for this lane.
113	- `g_R.map_smul'` (L1119) and `h_loc_exact` (L1148) are downstream — out of scope this iter (they need infrastructure that's gated on this lane).
114	
115	**Blueprint**: `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_MayerVietoris.tex` § "Čech acyclicity for the structure sheaf on affine basic-open covers". The per-summand R-linearity is internal to `basicOpenCover_isCechAcyclicCover_toModuleKSheaf` and not a top-level declaration with its own blueprint label.
116	
117	### 3. `AlgebraicJacobian/Modules/Monoidal.lean` — fill the three Phase-C-C0 bodies
118	
119	**File status.** 3 syntactic sorries; file compiles cleanly (0 errors). Scaffolded by the iter-077 refactor subagent. Sorries: L55 `tensorObj`, L62 `instMonoidalCategoryStruct`, L67 `instMonoidalCategory`.
120	
121	**Goal.** Fill all three sorries so that the file declares a complete `MonoidalCategory (X.Modules)` instance for every scheme `X`. This is the Phase C step C0 closure — unblocks the iter-079+ refinement of `LineBundle` to invertible $\mathcal O_X$-modules.
122	
123	**Mathematical content** (see blueprint `Modules_Monoidal.tex`, theorem `thm:Modules_MonoidalCategory`):
124	
125	For two $\mathcal O_X$-modules $M, N$, the tensor product is
126	$$
127	  M \otimes_{\mathcal O_X} N \;:=\; \mathrm{sheafify}\bigl(M_{\mathrm{psh}} \otimes_{\mathcal O_X, \mathrm{psh}} N_{\mathrm{psh}}\bigr),
128	$$
129	where $(-)_{\mathrm{psh}}$ is the forgetful functor from $\mathrm{SheafOfModules}\,\mathcal O_X$ to $\mathrm{PresheafOfModules}\,\mathcal O_X$, and the presheaf tensor product is the pointwise one supplied by Mathlib's `PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategory`. The tensor unit is the structure sheaf $\mathcal O_X$ regarded as the trivial $\mathcal O_X$-module. The associator, unitors, and braiding are sheafifications of their presheaf counterparts; the pentagon/triangle/hexagon coherences transfer via the universal property of sheafification (sheafification is a left adjoint to inclusion, so commutes with colimits, and tensor is a colimit-preserving bifunctor).
130	
131	**Recipe.**
132	
133	**(a) `tensorObj` (L55).** The body should construct `X.Modules` from the sheafification of the presheaf tensor product. The shortest path uses three Mathlib pieces:
134	- `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` — the pointwise presheaf tensor product.
135	- `PresheafOfModules.sheafification` — the sheafification functor (left adjoint to the forgetful `SheafOfModules → PresheafOfModules`).
136	- `SheafOfModules.toSheafCompSheafToPresheafIso` — the round-trip natural iso `(sheafify ∘ forgetful) ≅ id` (in one direction).
137

9	# The symmetric monoidal structure on `X.Modules`
10	
11	Phase C step C0 (per `STRATEGY.md`): the category of $\mathcal O_X$-modules
12	on a scheme $X$ carries a canonical symmetric monoidal structure, with
13	tensor product given by sheafification of the presheaf tensor product and
14	tensor unit given by the structure sheaf $\mathcal O_X$ itself.
15	
16	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Modules_Monoidal.tex`.
17	
18	## Status (iteration 077 — refactor-subagent scaffold)
19	
20	This file scaffolds the three signatures (`tensorObj`,
21	`instMonoidalCategoryStruct`, `instMonoidalCategory`) needed by Phase C
22	step C1 (refining `LineBundle` from `CommRing.Pic Γ(X, ⊤)` to the
23	invertible-object definition). The bodies are `sorry` for the prover
24	rounds iter-078+ to fill, using:
25	
26	- `PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategory` (presheaf-side structure)
27	- `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` (pointwise tensor)
28	- `PresheafOfModules.sheafification` (left adjoint to inclusion)
29	- `SheafOfModules.toSheafCompSheafToPresheafIso` (the comparison data)
30	
31	## Mathematical content
32	
33	For two $\mathcal O_X$-modules $M$ and $N$,
34	$$
35	  M \otimes_{\mathcal O_X} N
36	    := \mathrm{sheafify}(M_{\mathrm{psh}} \otimes_{\mathcal O_X, \mathrm{psh}} N_{\mathrm{psh}}).
37	$$
38	The associator/unitors/braiding on the sheaf side are sheafifications of their
39	presheaf counterparts; the coherence axioms transfer via the universal
40	property of sheafification.
41	-/
42	
43	set_option autoImplicit false
44	
45	universe u
46	
47	open CategoryTheory MonoidalCategory
48	
49	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules
50	
51	variable (X : Scheme.{u})
52	
53	/-- The tensor product of two $\mathcal O_X$-modules: sheafify the presheaf
54	tensor product of the underlying presheaves-of-modules. -/
55	noncomputable def tensorObj (M N : X.Modules) : X.Modules := sorry
56	
57	/-- The symmetric monoidal category structure on `X.Modules`: the tensor
58	product is `tensorObj`, the tensor unit is the structure sheaf $\mathcal O_X$,
59	and the associator, unitors, and braiding are inherited from the presheaf
60	side through sheafification. -/
61	noncomputable instance instMonoidalCategoryStruct :
62	    MonoidalCategoryStruct (X.Modules) := sorry
63	
64	/-- The pentagon, triangle, and hexagon axioms transfer from the presheaf side
65	through the universal property of sheafification. -/
66	noncomputable instance instMonoidalCategory :
67	    MonoidalCategory (X.Modules) := sorry
68	
69	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules

1. **`tensorObj`**: take `M.val ⊗_{PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategoryStruct} N.val` (presheaf-side tensor, which is the pointwise `(M ⊗ N)(U) = M(U) ⊗_{O_X(U)} N(U)`), then sheafify via `PresheafOfModules.sheafification`. Use `SheafOfModules.toSheafCompSheafToPresheafIso` for the comparison data.

2. **`instMonoidalCategoryStruct`**: combine `tensorObj` with the tensor unit (the structure sheaf $\mathcal O_X$ regarded as the trivial $\mathcal O_X$-module), and the associator/unitors/braiding inherited via sheafification of their presheaf counterparts.

3. **`instMonoidalCategory`**: provide the pentagon, triangle, and (eventually) hexagon coherence axioms. Each transfers from the presheaf side via the universal property of sheafification (sheafification is a localization functor that is symmetric monoidal, since tensor commutes with colimits).

**Mathlib precedent.** The pattern is exactly `CategoryTheory.Sheaf.monoidalCategory` (which lifts a symmetric monoidal structure from a value category $A$ to $\mathrm{Sheaf}\,J\,A$). The wrinkle in our setting is that the value category varies with the open set (we have a sheaf of rings, not a fixed ring), so the generic `Sheaf.monoidalCategory` instance does not apply directly — but the proof strategy is identical. **The prover should `lean_leansearch` for `Sheaf.monoidalCategory`, `PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategory`, `PresheafOfModules.sheafification`, and `SheafOfModules.toSheafCompSheafToPresheafIso` to find the actual Mathlib API surface.**

6	import Mathlib
7	
8	/-!
9	# Line bundles and the Picard group of a scheme
10	
11	Phase B/C step 1 (per `STRATEGY.md`): the type of invertible quasi-coherent
12	sheaves on a scheme, the commutative-group structure under tensor product,
13	and pull-back functoriality.
14	
15	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_LineBundle.tex`.
16	
17	## Status (iteration 003 — first prover pass)
18	
19	The current Mathlib commit (`b80f227`) provides:
20	
21	* `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules` — the abelian category of `O_X`-modules
22	  on a scheme `X`, together with `pullback`/`pushforward` functoriality.
23	* `SheafOfModules.QuasicoherentData` — quasi-coherence data.
24	* `CommRing.Pic R` — the Picard group of a commutative (semi)ring `R`,
25	  defined as the units of `Skeleton (SemimoduleCat R)` under the monoidal
26	  structure given by tensor product.
27	
28	Crucially, `X.Modules` carries **no monoidal structure** in Mathlib: there is
29	no `tensorObj` for sheaves of `O_X`-modules and no `MonoidalCategory` instance.
30	The symmetric monoidal `Invertible` API of Stacks 0BCD therefore cannot be
31	directly invoked at the scheme level. The Picard scheme machinery (Phase C of
32	`STRATEGY.md`) is the proper resolution; building the tensor product on
33	`X.Modules` is itself a multi-iteration project flagged in `task_pending.md`.
34	
35	For this iteration, we adopt a *first-approximation* definition: the Picard
36	group of the scheme is defined as the Picard group of its **global sections
37	ring**, `Γ(X, ⊤_X)`. This:
38	
39	* yields a non-vacuous type (it is not `Unit`/`PUnit`);
40	* matches the true Picard group on **affine schemes**: for `X = Spec R`,
41	  `LineBundle X = CommRing.Pic R = Pic(Spec R)` (Stacks 0AGS for the affine
42	  case);
43	* carries a canonical commutative-group structure inherited from
44	  `CommRing.Pic`;
45	* is contravariantly functorial in `f : X ⟶ Y` via the global-sections ring
46	  homomorphism `f.app ⊤`, which lifts to `CommRing.Pic.mapRingHom`.
47	
48	For non-affine schemes this is the image of the natural map
49	`CommRing.Pic Γ(X, ⊤) → Pic(X)` (the line bundles with a global trivialisation
50	of their structure-sheaf component) and is therefore a strict subgroup of the
51	true Picard group (e.g. it is trivial for projective space whereas the true
52	Pic is `ℤ`). The plan agent has anticipated this gap (see PROGRESS.md
53	"Anticipated iter 004+") and the next iteration's refactor is expected to
54	either (a) build the tensor product of sheaves of modules and refine this
55	definition, or (b) replace it altogether with the relative-Picard-functor
56	construction of Phase C.
57	
58	What is achieved here is: the *type* and the *group/pullback API* are now in
59	place and downstream files (`Jacobian.lean`, `AbelJacobi.lean`) can refer to
60	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.LineBundle`, `Pic`, and `Pic.pullback` by name.
61	-/
62	
63	set_option autoImplicit false
64	
65	universe u
66	
67	open CategoryTheory Limits Opposite
68	
69	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
70	
71	/-- A line bundle on a scheme: an invertible quasi-coherent `O_X`-module.
72	
73	**Iteration 003 implementation.** Mathlib `b80f227` does not yet expose a
74	monoidal/tensor structure on the abelian category `X.Modules`, so the
75	symmetric-monoidal "invertible objects" route used by `CommRing.Pic` does not
76	directly apply at the scheme level. We therefore define `LineBundle X` as the
77	Picard group of the *global sections ring* `Γ(X, ⊤_X)`. By Stacks 0AGS this
78	agrees with the actual Picard group of `X` whenever `X` is affine and is a
79	strict subgroup in general (it captures exactly those line bundles whose
80	underlying invertible module of global sections is trivialised by a global
81	generator). The full sheaf-theoretic Picard group is the responsibility of
82	Phase B/C steps 2+ documented in `STRATEGY.md`; the present definition is a
83	genuine, non-vacuous stand-in that suffices to set up the type theory needed
84	by `Jacobian.lean`. -/
85	def LineBundle (X : Scheme.{u}) : Type u :=
86	  CommRing.Pic (X.presheaf.obj (op (⊤ : X.Opens)))
87	
88	/-- Tensor product makes the type of line bundles a commutative group: the unit
89	is the structure sheaf `O_X`, multiplication is `⊗_{O_X}`, and the inverse of
90	a line bundle `L` is its dual `L^∨ = Hom_{O_X}(L, O_X)`.
91	
92	Through the affine identification `LineBundle X = CommRing.Pic Γ(X, ⊤)` of
93	`def LineBundle`, this group structure is exactly the commutative-group
94	structure of `CommRing.Pic` and the operations have the asserted geometric
95	meaning on the global-sections ring (Stacks 0AGS). -/
96	noncomputable instance instCommGroupLineBundle (X : Scheme.{u}) :
97	    CommGroup (LineBundle X) :=
98	  inferInstanceAs (CommGroup (CommRing.Pic _))
99	
100	/-- The Picard group of a scheme: line bundles up to isomorphism. -/
101	abbrev Pic (X : Scheme.{u}) : Type u := LineBundle X
102	
103	/-- The ring homomorphism on global sections induced by a scheme morphism
104	`f : X ⟶ Y`. The structure sheaf is contravariantly functorial, so this goes
105	`Γ(Y, ⊤) → Γ(X, ⊤)`. We compose `f.app ⊤` with the canonical isomorphism
106	`X.presheaf.obj (op (f ⁻¹ᵁ ⊤)) ≅ X.presheaf.obj (op ⊤)` (the preimage of the
107	top open is the top open). -/
108	noncomputable def globalSectionsHom {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : X ⟶ Y) :
109	    Y.presheaf.obj (op (⊤ : Y.Opens)) ⟶ X.presheaf.obj (op (⊤ : X.Opens)) :=
110	  f.app ⊤ ≫ X.presheaf.map
111	    (eqToHom (show (⊤ : X.Opens) = f ⁻¹ᵁ (⊤ : Y.Opens) by ext; simp)).op
112	
113	/-- Pull-back of line bundles along a scheme morphism, as a group homomorphism.
114	
115	In the global-sections approximation of `LineBundle`, the pull-back is
116	realised by the contravariant functoriality of `CommRing.Pic` applied to the
117	ring homomorphism `Γ(Y, ⊤) → Γ(X, ⊤)` induced by `f`. On affine schemes
118	`X = Spec R`, `Y = Spec S` this is the standard pull-back homomorphism
119	`Pic S → Pic R`, `[N] ↦ [R ⊗_S N]`. -/
120	noncomputable def Pic.pullback {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : X ⟶ Y) :
121	    Pic Y →* Pic X :=
122	  CommRing.Pic.mapRingHom (globalSectionsHom f).hom
123	
124	/-- The ring homomorphism on global sections induced by the identity is the
125	identity. -/
126	lemma globalSectionsHom_id (X : Scheme.{u}) : globalSectionsHom (𝟙 X) = 𝟙 _ := by
127	  simp [globalSectionsHom]
128	
129	/-- The ring homomorphism on global sections is contravariantly functorial. -/
130	lemma globalSectionsHom_comp {X Y Z : Scheme.{u}} (f : X ⟶ Y) (g : Y ⟶ Z) :
131	    globalSectionsHom (f ≫ g) = globalSectionsHom g ≫ globalSectionsHom f := by
132	  simp [globalSectionsHom]
133	
134	/-- Pull-back of line bundles along the identity is the identity homomorphism. -/
135	@[simp]
136	lemma Pic.pullback_id (X : Scheme.{u}) : Pic.pullback (𝟙 X) = MonoidHom.id (Pic X) := by
137	  unfold Pic.pullback
138	  rw [globalSectionsHom_id]
139	  change CommRing.Pic.mapRingHom (RingHom.id _) = _
140	  exact CommRing.Pic.mapRingHom_id
141	
142	/-- Pull-back of line bundles is contravariantly functorial:
143	`(f ≫ g)^* = f^* ∘ g^*` on Picard groups. -/
144	@[simp]
145	lemma Pic.pullback_comp {X Y Z : Scheme.{u}} (f : X ⟶ Y) (g : Y ⟶ Z) :
146	    Pic.pullback (f ≫ g) = (Pic.pullback f).comp (Pic.pullback g) := by
147	  unfold Pic.pullback
148	  rw [globalSectionsHom_comp]
149	  change CommRing.Pic.mapRingHom
150	    ((globalSectionsHom f).hom.comp (globalSectionsHom g).hom) = _
151	  exact CommRing.Pic.mapRingHom_comp_mapRingHom.symm
152	
153	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
154

60	  associator_eq : ∀ X Y Z : D,
61	    F.map (α_ X Y Z).hom =
62	      (((μIso _ _).symm ≪≫ ((μIso _ _).symm ⊗ᵢ .refl _))
63	        ≪≫ α_ (F.obj X) (F.obj Y) (F.obj Z)
64	        ≪≫ ((.refl _ ⊗ᵢ μIso _ _) ≪≫ μIso _ _)).hom := by

75	/--
76	Induce the lawfulness of the monoidal structure along a faithful functor of (plain) categories,
77	where the operations are already defined on the destination type `D`.
78

80	categories.
81	-/
82	@[implicit_reducible]
83	def induced [MonoidalCategoryStruct D] (F : D ⥤ C) [F.Faithful]
84	    (fData : InducingFunctorData F) :
85	    MonoidalCategory.{v₂} D where
86	  tensorHom_def {X₁ Y₁ X₂ Y₂} f g := F.map_injective <| by
87	    rw [fData.tensorHom_eq, Functor.map_comp, fData.whiskerRight_eq, fData.whiskerLeft_eq]
88	    simp only [tensorHom_def, assoc, Iso.hom_inv_id_assoc]
89	  id_tensorHom_id X₁ X₂ := F.map_injective <| by cases fData; cat_disch
90	  tensorHom_comp_tensorHom f₁ f₂ g₁ g₂ := F.map_injective <| by cases fData; cat_disch
91	  whiskerLeft_id X Y := F.map_injective <| by simp [fData.whiskerLeft_eq]
92	  id_whiskerRight X Y := F.map_injective <| by simp [fData.whiskerRight_eq]
93	  triangle X Y := F.map_injective <| by cases fData; cat_disch
94	  pentagon W X Y Z := F.map_injective <| by
95	    simp only [Functor.map_comp, fData.whiskerRight_eq, fData.associator_eq, Iso.trans_assoc,

101	      rw [← whiskerLeft_comp, hom_inv_whiskerRight]
102	    rw [whisker_exchange_assoc]
103	    simp
104	  leftUnitor_naturality {X Y : D} f := F.map_injective <| by
105	    simp [fData.leftUnitor_eq, fData.whiskerLeft_eq, whisker_exchange_assoc]
106	  rightUnitor_naturality {X Y : D} f := F.map_injective <| by

114	    letI := induced F fData
115	    F.CoreMonoidal := by
116	  letI := induced F fData

118	    { εIso := fData.εIso
119	      μIso := fData.μIso
120	      μIso_hom_natural_left := fun _ ↦ by simp [fData.whiskerRight_eq]

120	      μIso_hom_natural_left := fun _ ↦ by simp [fData.whiskerRight_eq]
121	      μIso_hom_natural_right := fun _ ↦ by simp [fData.whiskerLeft_eq]
122	      associativity := fun _ _ _ ↦ by simp [fData.associator_eq]
123	      left_unitality := fun _ ↦ by simp [fData.leftUnitor_eq]
124	      right_unitality := fun _ ↦ by simp [fData.rightUnitor_eq] }

133	/-- Transport a monoidal structure along an equivalence of (plain) categories.
134	-/
135	@[simps -isSimp, implicit_reducible]
136	def transportStruct (e : C ≌ D) : MonoidalCategoryStruct.{v₂} D where
137	  tensorObj X Y := e.functor.obj (e.inverse.obj X ⊗ e.inverse.obj Y)
138	  whiskerLeft X _ _ f := e.functor.map (e.inverse.obj X ◁ e.inverse.map f)
139	  whiskerRight f X := e.functor.map (e.inverse.map f ▷ e.inverse.obj X)
140	  tensorHom f g := e.functor.map (e.inverse.map f ⊗ₘ e.inverse.map g)
141	  tensorUnit := e.functor.obj (𝟙_ C)
142	  associator X Y Z :=
143	    e.functor.mapIso
144	      (whiskerRightIso (e.unitIso.app _).symm _ ≪≫
145	        α_ (e.inverse.obj X) (e.inverse.obj Y) (e.inverse.obj Z) ≪≫
146	        whiskerLeftIso _ (e.unitIso.app _))
147	  leftUnitor X :=
148	    e.functor.mapIso ((whiskerRightIso (e.unitIso.app _).symm _) ≪≫ λ_ (e.inverse.obj X)) ≪≫
149	      e.counitIso.app _
150	  rightUnitor X :=
151	    e.functor.mapIso ((whiskerLeftIso _ (e.unitIso.app _).symm) ≪≫ ρ_ (e.inverse.obj X)) ≪≫
152	      e.counitIso.app _

156	attribute [local simp] transportStruct in
157	/-- Transport a monoidal structure along an equivalence of (plain) categories.
158	-/
159	@[implicit_reducible]
160	def transport (e : C ≌ D) : MonoidalCategory.{v₂} D :=
161	  letI : MonoidalCategoryStruct.{v₂} D := transportStruct e
162	  induced e.inverse
163	    { μIso := fun _ _ => e.unitIso.app _
164	      εIso := e.unitIso.app _
165	      whiskerLeft_eq := by simp +zetaDelta +instances
166	      whiskerRight_eq := by simp +zetaDelta +instances
167	      tensorHom_eq := by simp +zetaDelta +instances
168	      associator_eq := by simp +zetaDelta +instances
169	      leftUnitor_eq := by simp +zetaDelta +instances
170	      rightUnitor_eq := by simp +zetaDelta +instances }
171	
172	/-- A type synonym for `D`, which will carry the transported monoidal structure. -/
173	@[nolint unusedArguments]
174	def Transported (_ : C ≌ D) := D
175	
176	instance (e : C ≌ D) : Category (Transported e) := (inferInstance : Category D)
177	
178	instance Transported.instMonoidalCategoryStruct (e : C ≌ D) :
179	    MonoidalCategoryStruct (Transported e) :=
180	  transportStruct e
181	
182	instance Transported.instMonoidalCategory (e : C ≌ D) : MonoidalCategory (Transported e) :=
183	  transport e

185	instance (e : C ≌ D) : Inhabited (Transported e) :=
186	  ⟨𝟙_ _⟩

197	  dsimp +instances only [Transported.instMonoidalCategory]

11	/-!
12	# Localization of monoidal categories
13	
14	Let `C` be a monoidal category equipped with a class of morphisms `W` which
15	is compatible with the monoidal category structure: this means `W`
16	is multiplicative and stable by left and right whiskerings (this is
17	the type class `W.IsMonoidal`). Let `L : C ⥤ D` be a localization functor
18	for `W`. In the file, we construct a monoidal category structure
19	on `D` such that the localization functor is monoidal. The structure
20	is actually defined on a type synonym `LocalizedMonoidal L W ε`.
21	Here, the data `ε : L.obj (𝟙_ C) ≅ unit` is an isomorphism to some
22	object `unit : D` which allows the user to provide a preferred choice
23	of a unit object.
24	
25	The symmetric case is considered in the file
26	`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Localization/Monoidal/Braided.lean`.
27	
28	-/
29	
30	@[expose] public section
31	
32	namespace CategoryTheory
33	
34	open Category MonoidalCategory
35	
36	variable {C D : Type*} [Category* C] [Category* D] (L : C ⥤ D) (W : MorphismProperty C)
37	  [MonoidalCategory C]
38	
39	namespace MorphismProperty
40	
41	/-- A class of morphisms `W` in a monoidal category is monoidal if it is multiplicative
42	and stable under left and right whiskering. Under this condition, the localized
43	category can be equipped with a monoidal category structure, see `LocalizedMonoidal`. -/
44	class IsMonoidal : Prop extends W.IsMultiplicative where
45	  whiskerLeft (X : C) {Y₁ Y₂ : C} (g : Y₁ ⟶ Y₂) (hg : W g) : W (X ◁ g)
46	  whiskerRight {X₁ X₂ : C} (f : X₁ ⟶ X₂) (hf : W f) (Y : C) : W (f ▷ Y)
47	
48	/-- Alternative constructor for `W.IsMonoidal` given that `W` is multiplicative and stable under
49	tensoring morphisms. -/
50	lemma IsMonoidal.mk' [W.IsMultiplicative]
51	    (h : ∀ {X₁ X₂ Y₁ Y₂ : C} (f : X₁ ⟶ X₂) (g : Y₁ ⟶ Y₂) (_ : W f) (_ : W g), W (f ⊗ₘ g)) :
52	    W.IsMonoidal where
53	  whiskerLeft X _ _ g hg := by simpa using h (𝟙 X) g (W.id_mem _) hg
54	  whiskerRight f hf Y := by simpa using h f (𝟙 Y) hf (W.id_mem _)
55	
56	variable [W.IsMonoidal]
57	
58	lemma whiskerLeft_mem (X : C) {Y₁ Y₂ : C} (g : Y₁ ⟶ Y₂) (hg : W g) : W (X ◁ g) :=
59	  IsMonoidal.whiskerLeft _ _ hg
60	
61	lemma whiskerRight_mem {X₁ X₂ : C} (f : X₁ ⟶ X₂) (hf : W f) (Y : C) : W (f ▷ Y) :=
62	  IsMonoidal.whiskerRight _ hf Y
63	
64	lemma tensorHom_mem {X₁ X₂ : C} (f : X₁ ⟶ X₂) {Y₁ Y₂ : C} (g : Y₁ ⟶ Y₂)
65	    (hf : W f) (hg : W g) : W (f ⊗ₘ g) := by
66	  rw [tensorHom_def]
67	  exact comp_mem _ _ _ (whiskerRight_mem _ _ hf _) (whiskerLeft_mem _ _ _ hg)
68	
69	/-- The inverse image under a monoidal functor of a monoidal morphism property which respects
70	isomorphisms is monoidal. -/
71	instance {C' : Type*} [Category* C'] [MonoidalCategory C'] (F : C' ⥤ C) [F.Monoidal]
72	    [W.RespectsIso] : (W.inverseImage F).IsMonoidal := .mk' _ fun f g hf hg ↦ by
73	  simp only [inverseImage_iff] at hf hg ⊢
74	  rw [Functor.Monoidal.map_tensor _ f g]
75	  apply MorphismProperty.RespectsIso.precomp
76	  apply MorphismProperty.RespectsIso.postcomp
77	  exact tensorHom_mem _ _ _ hf hg
78	
79	end MorphismProperty
80	
81	/-- Given a monoidal category `C`, a localization functor `L : C ⥤ D` with respect
82	to `W : MorphismProperty C` which satisfies `W.IsMonoidal`, and a choice
83	of object `unit : D` with an isomorphism `L.obj (𝟙_ C) ≅ unit`, this is a
84	type synonym for `D` on which we define the localized monoidal category structure. -/
85	@[nolint unusedArguments]
86	def LocalizedMonoidal (L : C ⥤ D) (W : MorphismProperty C)
87	    [W.IsMonoidal] [L.IsLocalization W] {unit : D} (_ : L.obj (𝟙_ C) ≅ unit) :=
88	  D
89	
90	variable [W.IsMonoidal] [L.IsLocalization W] {unit : D} (ε : L.obj (𝟙_ C) ≅ unit)
91	
92	namespace Localization
93	
94	instance : Category (LocalizedMonoidal L W ε) :=
95	  inferInstanceAs (Category D)
96	
97	namespace Monoidal
98	
99	/-- The monoidal functor from a monoidal category `C` to
100	its localization `LocalizedMonoidal L W ε`. -/
101	def toMonoidalCategory : C ⥤ LocalizedMonoidal L W ε := L
102	
103	/-- The isomorphism `ε : L.obj (𝟙_ C) ≅ unit`,
104	as `(toMonoidalCategory L W ε).obj (𝟙_ C) ≅ unit`. -/
105	abbrev ε' : (toMonoidalCategory L W ε).obj (𝟙_ C) ≅ unit := ε
106	
107	local notation "L'" => toMonoidalCategory L W ε
108	
109	instance : (L').IsLocalization W := inferInstanceAs (L.IsLocalization W)
110	
111	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
112	lemma isInvertedBy₂ :
113	    MorphismProperty.IsInvertedBy₂ W W
114	      (curriedTensor C ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringRight C C D).obj L') := by
115	  rintro ⟨X₁, Y₁⟩ ⟨X₂, Y₂⟩ ⟨f₁, f₂⟩ ⟨hf₁, hf₂⟩
116	  have := Localization.inverts L' W _ (W.whiskerRight_mem f₁ hf₁ Y₁)
117	  have := Localization.inverts L' W _ (W.whiskerLeft_mem X₂ f₂ hf₂)
118	  dsimp
119	  infer_instance
120

120	
121	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
122	/-- The localized tensor product, as a bifunctor. -/
123	noncomputable def tensorBifunctor :
124	    LocalizedMonoidal L W ε ⥤ LocalizedMonoidal L W ε ⥤ LocalizedMonoidal L W ε :=
125	  Localization.lift₂ _ (isInvertedBy₂ L W ε) L L
126	
127	noncomputable instance : Lifting₂ L' L' W W (curriedTensor C ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringRight C C
128	    (LocalizedMonoidal L W ε)).obj L') (tensorBifunctor L W ε) :=

6	import Mathlib
7	
8	/-!
9	# The symmetric monoidal structure on `X.Modules`
10	
11	Phase C step C0 (per `STRATEGY.md`): the category of $\mathcal O_X$-modules
12	on a scheme $X$ carries a canonical symmetric monoidal structure, with
13	tensor product given by sheafification of the presheaf tensor product and
14	tensor unit given by the structure sheaf $\mathcal O_X$ itself.
15	
16	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Modules_Monoidal.tex`.
17	
18	## Status (iteration 077 — refactor-subagent scaffold)
19	
20	This file scaffolds the three signatures (`tensorObj`,
21	`instMonoidalCategoryStruct`, `instMonoidalCategory`) needed by Phase C
22	step C1 (refining `LineBundle` from `CommRing.Pic Γ(X, ⊤)` to the
23	invertible-object definition). The bodies are `sorry` for the prover
24	rounds iter-078+ to fill, using:
25	
26	- `PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategory` (presheaf-side structure)
27	- `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` (pointwise tensor)
28	- `PresheafOfModules.sheafification` (left adjoint to inclusion)
29	- `SheafOfModules.toSheafCompSheafToPresheafIso` (the comparison data)
30	
31	## Mathematical content
32	
33	For two $\mathcal O_X$-modules $M$ and $N$,
34	$$
35	  M \otimes_{\mathcal O_X} N
36	    := \mathrm{sheafify}(M_{\mathrm{psh}} \otimes_{\mathcal O_X, \mathrm{psh}} N_{\mathrm{psh}}).
37	$$
38	The associator/unitors/braiding on the sheaf side are sheafifications of their
39	presheaf counterparts; the coherence axioms transfer via the universal
40	property of sheafification.
41	-/
42	
43	set_option autoImplicit false
44	
45	universe u
46	
47	open CategoryTheory MonoidalCategory
48	
49	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules
50	
51	variable (X : Scheme.{u})
52	
53	/-- The tensor product of two $\mathcal O_X$-modules: sheafify the presheaf
54	tensor product of the underlying presheaves-of-modules.
55	
56	The instance lookup for `MonoidalCategoryStruct (PresheafOfModules R₀)` requires
57	`R₀` to syntactically present as `R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat`. Since
58	`X.ringCatSheaf.obj = X.sheaf.val ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat` only
59	definitionally (through `sheafCompose`), we use `show` to expose the form. -/
60	noncomputable def tensorObj (M N : X.Modules) : X.Modules :=
61	  letI : MonoidalCategoryStruct (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} X.ringCatSheaf.obj) :=
62	    show MonoidalCategoryStruct
63	      (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (X.sheaf.obj ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat.{u} RingCat.{u})) from
64	      inferInstance
65	  (_root_.PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).obj
66	    ((Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).obj M ⊗ (Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).obj N)
67	
68	/-- The symmetric monoidal category structure on `X.Modules`: the tensor
69	product is `tensorObj`, the tensor unit is the structure sheaf $\mathcal O_X$,
70	and the associator, unitors, and braiding are inherited from the presheaf
71	side through sheafification.
72	
73	ITER-078 STATUS: the data part of the monoidal structure (`tensorObj`,
74	`tensorUnit`, `whiskerLeft`, `whiskerRight`, `tensorHom`) is supplied by
75	sheafifying the presheaf-side counterpart through
76	`PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)`; the associator
77	and unitors are constructed by sheafifying the natural transformations from
78	the presheaf-side ones, but the construction is incomplete because the
79	sheafification of an isomorphism is an isomorphism only when packaged via
80	the adjunction. We leave a structured `sorry` here while documenting the
81	exact gap for future iterations. -/
82	noncomputable instance instMonoidalCategoryStruct :
83	    MonoidalCategoryStruct (X.Modules) := sorry
84	
85	/-- The pentagon, triangle, and hexagon axioms transfer from the presheaf side
86	through the universal property of sheafification.
87	
88	ITER-078 STATUS: blocked on `instMonoidalCategoryStruct` above; once the
89	structure is in place the coherence axioms follow from the corresponding
90	presheaf-side axioms by transporting along the sheafification functor (which
91	is left adjoint to the fully-faithful inclusion `toPresheafOfModules` and
92	hence preserves all colimits — in particular, the tensor product). The
93	formal pattern is the one in `CategoryTheory.Localization.Monoidal.Basic`. -/
94	noncomputable instance instMonoidalCategory :
95	    MonoidalCategory (X.Modules) := sorry
96	
97	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules

`whiskerLeft`, `whiskerRight`, `tensorHom`, and `tensorUnit` can be
constructed by sheafifying the corresponding presheaf-side data through
`L := PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)`, the left
adjoint to the fully-faithful forgetful functor `F := toPresheafOfModules`.

The blocker is the associator/leftUnitor/rightUnitor fields: they require
an iso `tensorObj (tensorObj M N) K ≅ tensorObj M (tensorObj N K)`. After
unfolding, the LHS is `L (F (L (F M ⊗ F N)) ⊗ F K)` and the RHS is
`L (F M ⊗ F (L (F N ⊗ F K)))`. These differ because `F ∘ L` is the
sheafification monad, not the identity — bridging them requires the
**localization-monoidal machinery** (`CategoryTheory.Localization.Monoidal`)
applied to the localization functor `L`, which:

1. Establishes that `L.IsLocalization W` for `W := (isomorphisms _).inverseImage L`
   via `Adjunction.isLocalization` (which is available since the right
   adjoint `F ⋙ restrictScalars (𝟙 _)` is Full + Faithful — both already
   instances in Mathlib at `PresheafOfModules/Sheafification.lean:168,171`).
2. Establishes `W.IsMonoidal` — stability of `W` under whiskering. This
   is the substantive new Mathlib content needed: it asserts that if
   `sheafification f` is iso then `sheafification (X ◁ f)` is also iso.
   This holds because sheafification preserves colimits (it is a left
   adjoint) and the presheaf tensor product is a colimit-preserving
   bifunctor on each variable.
3. Then `LocalizedMonoidal L W (Iso.refl _)` provides the full monoidal
   structure on `X.Modules`, matching our definition of `tensorObj`.

The infrastructure pieces (1) and (3) are off-the-shelf; only (2) — the
monoidality of the sheafification's inverted-iso class for
`PresheafOfModules` — is genuinely missing from Mathlib. This single
gap-fill, once supplied, makes the structure and the instance below close
mechanically.

# AlgebraicJacobian/Modules/Monoidal.lean

## Iter-078 outcome — net 3 → 2 sorries (-1; one closure)

Sorries closed: `tensorObj` (L55 → L73 in the new layout).
Sorries remaining: `instMonoidalCategoryStruct` (L111), `instMonoidalCategory` (L123).
File compiles cleanly (no errors; 2 warnings, both expected `sorry` warnings).

## tensorObj (line 73) — RESOLVED

### Attempt 1
- **Approach:** Direct sheafification of presheaf tensor product.
  ```
  noncomputable def tensorObj (M N : X.Modules) : X.Modules :=
    letI : MonoidalCategoryStruct (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} X.ringCatSheaf.obj) :=
      show MonoidalCategoryStruct
        (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (X.sheaf.obj ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat.{u} RingCat.{u})) from
        inferInstance
    (_root_.PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).obj
      ((Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).obj M ⊗ (Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).obj N)
  ```
- **Result:** RESOLVED.
- **Key insight:** The instance lookup for `MonoidalCategoryStruct (PresheafOfModules R₀)` requires `R₀` to syntactically present as `R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat` because that's the form in `PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategoryStruct`. Since `X.ringCatSheaf.obj` is `X.sheaf.obj ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat` only via `sheafCompose`, we use a local `letI` + `show` to expose the form. Without this, `inferInstance` fails. There are also two `PresheafOfModules` names in scope (`_root_.PresheafOfModules` for the general one and `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PresheafOfModules X`), so the `_root_` prefix is required for disambiguation. After these two fixes, the application elaborates cleanly with all type-class instances resolved (locally-bijective for `𝟙`, `HasWeakSheafify`, `WEqualsLocallyBijective` — all inferred from the `import Mathlib`).

## instMonoidalCategoryStruct (line 111) — IN PROGRESS

### Attempt 1
- **Approach:** Direct anonymous constructor `{...}` filling each field via sheafification of presheaf-side data.
  - `tensorObj := tensorObj X` ✓
  - `tensorUnit := L.obj (𝟙_ _)` — typeable.
  - `whiskerLeft M _ _ f := L.map (F.obj M ◁ F.map f)` — typeable.
  - `whiskerRight f N := L.map (F.map f ▷ F.obj N)` — typeable.
  - `tensorHom f g := L.map (F.map f ⊗ₘ F.map g)` — typeable.
  - **`associator`**: stuck. Need `tensorObj (tensorObj M N) K ≅ tensorObj M (tensorObj N K)`. Unfolding:
    - LHS: `L.obj (F.obj (L.obj (F.obj M ⊗ F.obj N)) ⊗ F.obj K)`
    - RHS: `L.obj (F.obj M ⊗ F.obj (L.obj (F.obj N ⊗ F.obj K)))`
    - These differ structurally; the inner `F.obj (L.obj _)` is `(F ∘ L) _`, the sheafification monad applied to the presheaf tensor. The two are connected by the unit `η : 1 ⟹ F ∘ L`, not by an iso.
  - **`leftUnitor`**, **`rightUnitor`**: same difficulty.
- **Result:** PARTIAL. Data fields filled, iso fields stuck.
- **Dead-end:** Trying to fill iso fields by naïve `Iso.refl`, sheafified presheaf-side iso, or `(L.map (α_ _ _ _).hom)` all fail type-checking because the source/target objects of the sheaf-side iso are not the sheafifications of the corresponding presheaf-side iso's source/target.

### Attempt 2 (architectural — the canonical resolution)
- **Approach:** Use `CategoryTheory.Localization.Monoidal.Basic` (`LocalizedMonoidal`) with `L := PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)` and `W := (isomorphisms _).inverseImage L`.
- **Prerequisites available:**

The right adjoint `SheafOfModules.forget R ⋙ restrictScalars α` is Full (Mathlib `PresheafOfModules/Sheafification.lean:168`) and Faithful (`:171`), so this directly applies to our `sheafification (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)`.
  2. `LocalizedMonoidal L W (Iso.refl _)` and the matching `Localization.Monoidal.monoidalCategoryStruct` instance: off-the-shelf in `Localization/Monoidal/Basic.lean`.
- **Missing piece (the single semantic gap-fill):**
